B. Denoising Diffusion Model for Pedestrian
T rajectory Prediction
Diffusion models have succeeded substantially across var-
ious generative tasks [30]. In the context of trajectory
prediction, one notable advantage of using this approach lies
in its capacity to conceptualize the reduction of prediction
uncertainty as an inverse diffusion process [24]. Owing to
the inherent inclusion of a substantial number of diffusion
steps within conventional diffusion models, their application
to predicting multiagent human trajectories for vulnerable
pedestrians necessitates a considerable expenditure of com-
putational resources. Hence, we present the UTD-PTP model
in this study, depicted in the encoder block of Fig. 1. The
diffusion step has been condensed into two steps to mitigate
the computational resource overhead. This section will first
elaborate on how to model the diffusion process and the
inverse diffusion process using the ordinary diffusion model.
It will then explain how to significantly reduce the diffusion
steps based on the ordinary diffusion model through the
principles of DDIM.
1) Diffusion Process for Pedestrian T rajectory: Given a
human trajectory X, the diffusion process can be mathemati-
cally formulated as follows :
